date_notes: Nature magazine documents muzzling policy July 2013; practice documented from 2007 onward.
title_en: Federal government media protocols restrict scientists from speaking publicly without prior approval
title_fr: Des protocoles médiatiques fédéraux empêchent les scientifiques de parler publiquement sans autorisation préalable
description_en: The Harper government's media protocol requires federal scientists to obtain government approval before speaking to journalists. The policy particularly affects climate, fisheries, and environmental researchers. Thousands of positions are eliminated and research libraries are closed during this period.

relations.note: Muzzling and library closures are concurrent mechanisms of the same pattern.
